## [27:48 - 30:47]

The second thing I want to point out is the order form. One test that I run pretty much all the time with clients is moving the order form up and above the full. And so above the full is just like an old school term from like back in the newspaper day which means like above the fold of how the newspapers folded. Every single time I have the order form above kind of like on the first screen without any scrolling the conversions are better. So a lot of people run their order forms like down here and you have to scroll it on to see it. I've never been able to beat this format where the order form is just up here at the top. And a couple of things about the order form that I kind of want to point out. So order form should always go straight up and down. You never want to go bouncing across. Some people do this for like first name will be here last name will be there. You need to make it as simple as possible. So that'll go back to one of the things we talked about here when I kind of break down what really works on order forms. But straight up and down is exactly what you want. And the other big thing is get rid of any fields that you don't need. So phone numbers probably the biggest one that can make a difference. If you're doing outbound calls and you're doing some type of sales follow up you probably need the phone number.
